introduction: Welcome to the ENG192 Robotics Lab! I'm Jackie, and I'll be your guide today. Let's explore what we do here. Follow me!
conclusion: That concludes our lab tour! I hope you enjoyed learning about our robotics research. Feel free to ask me any questions — just head back to the home screen and tap Ask Jackie!

stops.name: Lab Entrance
stops.narration: This is the BioRob Lab, part of SJSU's Mechanical Engineering department. We focus on robotics, human-robot interaction, and biomedical devices. The lab is led by Professor Kazerooni and supports both undergraduate and graduate research.

stops.name: Robot Workstation
stops.narration: Here's where we build and program our robots. You're looking at workstations equipped with ROS, sensor arrays, and embedded computing platforms. Students work on everything from navigation algorithms to computer vision.

stops.name: 3D Printing Area
stops.narration: Our 3D printing station handles rapid prototyping for robot parts, custom sensor mounts, and mechanical fixtures. We use both FDM and resin printers for different precision needs.

stops.name: Testing Area
stops.narration: This is our testing zone where we validate robot behaviors in controlled conditions before deploying them in real-world scenarios. We test navigation, object recognition, and human-robot interaction protocols here.
